materialChanges: Corrected the worked ideal-gas audit to classify R as the universal physical constant in compatible units and to state explicitly that the prediction adjusts no model parameter.
Aligned both stale version statements in ATTRIBUTION.md with lesson version 0.1.1 and updated the finalization summary to describe this post-review revision accurately.

reviewDispositions.rationale: The central worked-audit table now labels R as the universal physical constant expressed in units compatible with L, kPa, mol, and K, and adds that no model parameter is adjusted in this prediction. The concept and practice scenes continue to teach genuine adjustable or fitted model parameters.

The reviewer independently confirmed the ideal-gas, sensitivity, polynomial, feeder, and chamber calculations and the bounded answer logic. The final revision does not alter any of those values or keyed conclusions.

The supported claim set, uncertainty boundaries, reference records, and complete same-scene source-note mappings remain intact; only the incorrect R classification identified in finding 0 required correction.
Both stale 0.1.0 statements in ATTRIBUTION.md now identify this pack as version 0.1.1, matching lesson.json and all structured pack metadata.
The prerequisite diagnostic and its non-punitive prediction-frame recovery route already provide the praised constructive alignment and remain unchanged.
The worked example retains its full ten-step reasoning sequence and self-explanation prompt. Correcting one table classification strengthens rather than removes that instructional structure.
The misconception sequence still elicits a prediction, demonstrates the failure of the shortcut, repairs the inference, and rechecks it with measurement uncertainty.
The chamber task already requires transfer of the audit to changed scale, mixing, spatial sampling, and purpose, and correctly distinguishes a sampled-point mean from a chamber-volume average.
All four assessment items remain mapped to the declared objectives and use changed contexts and values with misconception-sensitive answer logic, rubrics, retry variants, and remediation.

evidenceChecked: All six ordered scene files have one level-one heading, linear source order, ordinary semantic headings, and no dependence on fixed viewport position, color, sound, motion, hover, dragging, or timing.

The model-use diagram has matching concise alt text and complete long descriptions in its inert local diagram JSON and learner-facing directive; the adjacent table and prose preserve the same sequence and decision branches nonvisually.
Both ideal-gas equations are read in words, tables are explained in prose, and the chamber table has an explicit equivalent nonvisual route stating every value, aggregation limit, and conclusion.

Checks and transfer prompts use text responses followed by persistent source-order feedback; assessment is retakeable, has no time limit, and supplies reasoning, common-error recovery, alternative retry contexts, and no physical or sensory-only requirement.

Static inspection found no raw HTML, JavaScript, remote media or embeds, Markdown images, active SVG, executable notebook, path escape, data URL, unsafe interaction, secret, personal record, patient material, or clinical direction; the only visual source is bounded local diagram JSON.
All eight material claims resolve to the three declared sources and each claim-bearing scene exposes the complete supporting-source union in a learner-visible source note.

Every reference records factual-reference use, facts-only original expression, rights-basis evidence, dated agent-access status, and a terms URL; no source is marked human-only. The official EPA guidance and disclaimer, NASA equation-of-state and disclaimer, and NTRS record resolved during this audit.

EPA expression is not redistributed, NASA sources are used only for factual verification, the NTRS record identifies the technical publication as public with public use permitted, and the lesson declares no third-party assets or datasets.

ATTRIBUTION.md identifies the accountable principal, CC BY 4.0 course-content grant, original lesson prose/examples/assessment/diagram, source-access boundary, absence of third-party assets, authoring-agent disclosure, and the finalizer record in adjudication.json.
limitations: No rendered browser, screen-reader, keyboard-only, 200% zoom/reflow, reduced-motion, print, touch, or narrow-screen session was tested; accessibility judgment is based on the complete static Markdown, JSON, and assessment source.
No PDF, EPUB, DOCX, JATS, or other non-web export was rendered because those adapters are documented as unavailable; export parity was assessed from source-level equivalents.

The supplied scope prohibited reading local site schemas and running npm, so this run performed JSON parsing, relationship checks, directive and unsafe-source scans, and diff checks but not schema, strict source-preflight, or repository validation.
Official source and terms routes were resolved, but robots policies and the pack's dated no-agent-restriction observations were not independently rechecked during this run.

rationale: The original candidate and complete pack record were read, including the two older reviews that were excluded because they target lesson version 0.1.0 and candidate bd75204d63a87c704bd59bae0329c53d2db94590. The counted 0.1.1 academic and learning-design artifacts target the same original commit, declare conflicts, use distinct run IDs and declared providers, and carry eligible request-changes and approve verdicts. The operator states that equivalent non-dismissed GitHub transports are COMMENTED reviews; this run did not independently query GitHub and does not represent the disclosed provenance as verified independence. The academic blocker was corrected without changing the valid calculations, and the learning-design version mismatch was corrected while all seven affirmative findings were preserved. Static integrity checks found complete scene, objective, assessment, claim, source-note, source, and file relationships, and the final accessibility-and-rights audit is satisfied subject to its stated testing limits. No credible unresolved content, academic, accessibility, rights, privacy, security, or provenance blocker remains, so merge is warranted once the operational conditions below are completed.
dissent: The eligible academic reviewer issued request-changes because the candidate called R a model parameter. That material dissent is preserved here and resolved by the final correction identifying R as the universal physical constant and stating that the worked prediction adjusts no model parameter.

Both eligible reviews were operated by the accountable author principal github:VSBDev. The policy permits this founding-stage arrangement with isolated runs, but it is disclosed operator accountability rather than verified human independence.
conditions: After committing this exact final lesson content and the two cited review artifacts, replace PENDING-FINAL-COMMIT with that real 40-character commit SHA without changing teaching content.
Replace the four RUNTIME-STAMPED adjudicator agent-identity fields from the runtime banner before the final artifact is submitted.

Before merge, run the repository's required schema, strict source-preflight, and verification gates and verify that both counted COMMENTED GitHub submissions remain equivalent and non-dismissed; all must pass.
Reserve the publication transition to the maintainer and limit it to the permitted lesson status, source-confidence, and retained-claim review-status fields.
